item.title: SYSTEM ADMINISTRATION TUTORIAL SERIES - INTRO
item.description:
Radhe Radhe! This is going to be a beginner to advance of system administration tutorial. This whole section will contain my personal notes , those which i had learned during a bunch of sessions. If you feel any difficulty feel free to reach out through this mail cyb3rinsi6hts@gmail.com .

I hope you will enjoy this big tutorial. I am still learning , please pardon any mistakes. Thank you for your time.

item.title: Practical Wireshark
item.description: A practical guide to using Wireshark for network analysis.

item.title: Recon on myself
item.description:
Welcome. If you’re here, you’re likely passionate about red teaming and the art of the technical write-up. This blog is my contribution to that community. I appreciate you taking the time to be here.

What You’ll Find Here

Red Teaming Notes

I share real-world notes and tactics I’ve picked up through learning, labs, and exercises. Think of it as my personal “Field Manual” — from privilege escalation to lateral movement and beyond.

item.title: SYSTEM ADMINISTRATION TUTORIAL SERIES - Part 1
item.description:
Commands are indicated like this: COMMANDS

  1. purpose of touch command →

    1. creating a new file
    2. changing / modifying the timestamp of a file

  2. stat command is used to show the timestamp of any file in directory.

  3. touch {1..10}.txt → creating simultaneous file at sme time

  4. Creating recursive directories altogether → mkdir LPU/CSE LPU/ECE LPU/ME

  5. ls -R /LPU

  6. tty → terminal interface

  7. user info enumueration → id,whoami,who,id,w

  8. logname → to see current login name

item.title: Why i chose books over certs!
item.description:
Radhe Radhe!

This is going to be a very raw blog, mostly about what I am going to post in the next few months, and what I have been doing for the past few weeks.

So, let’s start with what I was doing…

After completing eJPT certification and learning the basics as well as core concepts of system administration, I got my hands dirty on CTF platforms like HTB. I took a one-month subscription just to get the experience of it. But not gonna lie, it was not what I expected. First of all, playing CTFs on these platforms are very good for many people, and the platforms do a great job. But I didn’t like the CTFish or more like obviously vulnerable experience. I thought the normal HTB boxes would replicate real-world scenarios—hardened systems where normal scans easily fail, and where initial access depends on phishing, social engineering, or hardcore OSINT rather than an obvious open port. That wasn’t what I found. But definately HTB is a great platform for sharpening your skills under time pressure.

item.title: Practical Snort
item.description: A practical guide to use Snort.

item.title: SYSTEM ADMINISTRATION TUTORIAL SERIES - Part 2
item.description:
1. which → Exact location of the exeutable file
  2. whereis → local documentation
  3. Linux help commands :
    1. man → manual pages , docs of the command → we must aware about the exact command name of the command
      1. Total 9 categories of the man pages are available
        1. exe , config , kernel files etc categories.
      2. If man doesnot work for newer systems we need to update the man databse → sudo mandb
      3. man man
      4. man -k ANY_COMMAND_YOU_KNOW_PARTIALLY like password
